title: nameservers in container's /etc/resolv.conf should be reachable
body:
When launching `podman run` without `--dns` option, host's `/etc/resolv.conf` is used for the container.

This is a problem when host's `/etc/resolv.conf` only contains nameservers not reachable from the container, e.g. 127.0.0.1.  

Docker ignores  127.0.0.1 in host's `/etc/resolv.conf`. When there’s no other nameserver, it adds 8.8.8.8 and 8.8.4.4.

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:
**kind bug**

comments.body:
Looks like docker's [resolvconf package](https://github.com/docker/libnetwork/tree/master/resolvconf) is doing all the job of filtering host's `resolv.conf`. 

Do you think that this could be imported in libpod ?
I'll look into it - we already have most of this implemented, so it might be easier to rework our existing code to do this
Using the command line switch for --dns is not desirable ?

@baude that's a possibility, but probably a user expects a docker-like user experience when using podman. 
Also passing `--dns` is not always an option (e.g. podman used in a script).
Going to take a look at this one today
